message:
fix(abi): nl_langinfo CODESET → UTF-8, sysconf SC_PHYS_PAGES/SC_ARG_MAX, 30+ new _SC_ values

Bug #13: nl_langinfo(CODESET) returned "ANSI_X3.4-1968" instead of "UTF-8".
Modern Linux defaults to UTF-8; the old value broke encoding detection in
programs that check nl_langinfo.

Bug #14: sysconf(_SC_PHYS_PAGES) returned EINVAL. Now reads MemTotal from
/proc/meminfo and converts to page count.

Bug #15: sysconf(_SC_ARG_MAX) was hardcoded to 2 MiB. Now calculated from
RLIMIT_STACK / 4 (matching glibc behavior), capped at 3200000.

Also added 30+ missing sysconf values: _SC_PHYS_PAGES, _SC_AVPHYS_PAGES,
_SC_NGROUPS_MAX, _SC_GETPW_R_SIZE_MAX, _SC_GETGR_R_SIZE_MAX,
_SC_LOGIN_NAME_MAX, _SC_TTY_NAME_MAX, _SC_SYMLOOP_MAX, _SC_RE_DUP_MAX,
_SC_VERSION, _SC_THREADS, _SC_THREAD_KEYS_MAX, _SC_THREAD_STACK_MIN,
_SC_THREAD_DESTRUCTOR_ITERATIONS, _SC_MONOTONIC_CLOCK, _SC_CPUTIME,
_SC_MAPPED_FILES, _SC_MEMLOCK, _SC_MEMORY_PROTECTION, _SC_SEMAPHORES,
_SC_SHARED_MEMORY_OBJECTS, _SC_SYNCHRONIZED_IO, _SC_TIMERS,
_SC_REALTIME_SIGNALS, _SC_PRIORITY_SCHEDULING, _SC_FSYNC, _SC_ASYNCHRONOUS_IO

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
